title: hydrangea fistulosa
intro: Hydrangea fistulosa, also known as Rough-leaved Hydrangea, is a perennial shrub native to the mountainous regions of Asia, characterized by its large, white or pale blue flowers and broad, toothed leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties, which can help alleviate urinary tract issues, fever, and rheumatism. The health benefits of Hydrangea fistulosa include its ability to reduce swelling, ease pain, and promote the removal of toxins from the body, making it a popular remedy for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of Hydrangea fistulosa can be found in the form of tea, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat a range of ailments when consumed under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
